description.p: Wayfinder Aura is a local-first voice dictation application for Linux. Press a hotkey, speak, and your words are typed wherever your cursor is. By default, transcription runs on your device with whisper.cpp — in local mode your voice never leaves your computer. Optional cloud transcription backends are available if you choose to enable them, and the app checks online for model updates (toggleable).

ul.li: Local-first & Private — transcribed on your device by default; optional cloud backends if you choose them
Hotkey Triggered — Press a key to start/stop recording
Wayland & X11 Support — Works on modern Linux desktops
System Tray Integration — Runs quietly in the background
Multiple Whisper Models — Choose speed vs accuracy (tiny → large)
Smart Cleanup — Optional local LLM polishes grammar and tone
Game Aware — Hotkeys pause automatically while a game is running
Configurable Typing Speed — Instant paste or simulated typing

description.p: Perfect for writing emails, documents, code comments, chat messages, or any situation where speaking is faster than typing.

screenshot.caption: Wayfinder Aura main interface showing recording status
Settings panel with model selection and hotkey configuration

description.p: First public release of Wayfinder Aura

ul.li: Local voice dictation with whisper.cpp — hotkey-triggered, Wayland and X11
Steam Deck support: SIGILL-safe CPU build, R4 trigger socket, tray, font and scaling fixes
Stable microphone selection by device name — survives PipeWire device renumbering
Game-aware hotkeys: dictation keys pause while a GameMode game is running
Default hotkeys Super+F2 / Super+F3 (avoid in-game F-key collisions)
Tone-aware local post-processing with Gemma 3 1B as the default model
System tray integration and configurable typing speeds
